a:r.a:t: Seven infrastructure modules provide the foundation for enterprise reliability. The RBAC service in authz/service.py at 269 lines implements principal and role membership tracking with tenant-scoped resource access control. It provides the foundation for extending to ABAC — attribute-based access control — for dataset and column-level checks. The memory v2 layer in persistence/postgres/memory_v2.py at 831 lines introduces MemoryEpisode and ManagedMemoryRecord types with importance and confidence scoring per entry, TTL hints for expiration, supersession tracking so corrected facts replace old ones, and provenance tracking back to source turns. Multi-scope support covers both conversation and user-level memories. Live progress and status tracking in the api directory provide real-time SSE status events and job progress updates. The status tracker grew to 827 lines to support per-tool-call granularity. Tool parallelism in tool_parallelism.py at 500 lines enables wave-based parallel tool execution with concurrency key conflict detection and a budget of 12 calls per turn — meaning multiple independent tool calls run simultaneously rather than sequentially. Prompt fallbacks at the package root provides resilient fallback prompts for all agent roles with a shared charter and per-role mission statements, so agents keep working even when skill files go missing. Task decomposition in task_decomposition.py at 341 lines handles mixed-intent request detection and routes them appropriately — if someone asks for arithmetic plus data analysis plus document search in one message, it splits that across the right specialists.
